speaker: CHORUS
text: O, for a muse of fire that would ascend

speaker: CHORUS
text: The brightest heaven of invention!

speaker: CHORUS
text: A kingdom for a stage, princes to act,

speaker: CHORUS
text: And monarchs to behold the swelling scene!

speaker: CHORUS
text: Then should the warlike Harry, like himself,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Assume the port of Mars, and at his heels,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Leashed in like hounds, should famine, sword, and fire

speaker: CHORUS
text: Crouch for employment. But pardon, gentles all,

speaker: CHORUS
text: The flat unraisèd spirits that hath dared

speaker: CHORUS
text: On this unworthy scaffold to bring forth

speaker: CHORUS
text: So great an object. Can this cockpit hold

speaker: CHORUS
text: The vasty fields of France? Or may we cram

speaker: CHORUS
text: Within this wooden O the very casques

speaker: CHORUS
text: That did affright the air at Agincourt?

speaker: CHORUS
text: O pardon, since a crookèd figure may

speaker: CHORUS
text: Attest in little place a million,

speaker: CHORUS
text: And let us, ciphers to this great account,

speaker: CHORUS
text: On your imaginary forces work.

speaker: CHORUS
text: Suppose within the girdle of these walls

speaker: CHORUS
text: Are now confined two mighty monarchies,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Whose high uprearèd and abutting fronts

speaker: CHORUS
text: The perilous narrow ocean parts asunder.

speaker: CHORUS
text: Piece out our imperfections with your thoughts.

speaker: CHORUS
text: Into a thousand parts divide one man,

speaker: CHORUS
text: And make imaginary puissance.

speaker: CHORUS
text: Think, when we talk of horses, that you see them

speaker: CHORUS
text: Printing their proud hoofs i’ th’ receiving earth,

speaker: CHORUS
text: For ’tis your thoughts that now must deck our kings,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Carry them here and there, jumping o’er times,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Turning th’ accomplishment of many years

speaker: CHORUS
text: Into an hourglass; for the which supply,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Admit me chorus to this history,

speaker: CHORUS
text: Who, prologue-like, your humble patience pray

speaker: CHORUS
text: Gently to hear, kindly to judge our play.
